headlines.title: BofA sees Japan sell-off risk as short yen positions stretch
Japan's crude run touches six-week high as crude stocks rebuild
Japan's Itochu and Daikin to recover aluminum from air conditioners
How Japan’s yen rout could spark a US financial crisis
Stock market today: Japan's Nikkei and South Korea's KOSPI fall amid valuation-driven AI sell-off
Japan’s Nikkei Stock Average Ends at near 4-Week Low as Tech Tracks Nasdaq Selloff (UPDATE 1)
Japanese Yen: Weak tone within defined trading band against US Dollar – UOB

marketTone.ko: 오늘 일본 증시는 기술주 중심의 매도세로 하락 마감하며 닛케이 평균주가가 약 4주 만에 최저치를 기록했습니다. 엔화 약세는 지속적인 우려 요인으로, 미 달러 대비 약세 기조를 유지하는 가운데 BofA는 엔화 숏 포지션 확대로 인한 일본 증시 매도 위험을 경고했습니다. 특히, 엔화 약세 장기화가 미국 금융 위기를 촉발할 수 있다는 분석까지 제기되며 시장 전반에 걸쳐 경계심이 고조되고 있습니다.
marketTone.en: Japanese equities saw a notable decline today, with the Nikkei Stock Average falling to a near four-week low, primarily driven by a valuation-driven sell-off in technology stocks tracking Nasdaq's performance. The weak yen remains a significant theme, with its 'weak tone' against the US dollar prompting concerns from BofA about potential sell-off risks as short yen positions stretch. Furthermore, headlines raised the notable catalyst that a prolonged yen rout could potentially spark a US financial crisis, adding to market caution.
